title: Reservoir Dogs
logline: After a jewelry heist goes catastrophically wrong, a group of color-coded criminals convene at a warehouse to figure out who set them up, while a gut-shot member bleeds out and paranoia turns deadly.
antagonistOrConflict: The traitor within the crew / Joe Cabot's suspicion of Mr. Orange
tone: Violent, darkly comic, paranoid, profane, stylized

actBreaks.description: Mr. Blonde's backstory with Joe establishes his loyalty and sets up his return to the warehouse with a hostage, escalating the situation.

validation.ruling: Scene #14 (p38, 40%) cuts away from the warehouse crisis to Joe's office where Toothpick Vic Vega (Mr. Blonde) is welcomed after prison. This is a major structural break—a hard cut to extended flashback that fundamentally shifts the narrative mode from present-tense thriller to backstory exposition, representing the end of Act 1's setup and beginning of Act 2's investigation into what went wrong.

actBreaks.description: Mr. Orange (Freddy) receives the call from Nice Guy Eddie to go to the job, marking his full commitment as an undercover cop entering the heist.

validation.ruling: Scene #36 (p73, 76%) shows the crew driving to the warehouse in Eddie's car, bantering casually about race and women. This marks the final transition before the climactic warehouse confrontation—the last moment of relative normalcy before all hell breaks loose. It functions as the calm before the storm that ends Act 2 and launches into the final act's resolution.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates as a fractured mosaic, beginning in medias res with the aftermath of disaster and then spiraling backward and forward through time to reveal how we got here and where we're going. The opening diner scene (scene 1) establishes the crew's dynamic before the job, then we jump violently into the bloody present (scenes 3-4) with Mr. Orange gut-shot in the warehouse. From there, Tarantino uses character-specific flashback chapters—Mr. Blonde's history with Joe, Mr. Orange's undercover preparation—to deepen our understanding while maintaining relentless forward momentum in the warehouse present. The structure creates a pressure-cooker effect: we're trapped in real-time with dying men and rising paranoia, while flashbacks provide context that makes the present even more unbearable. The non-linear approach isn't gimmickry; it's essential to the film's central tension between what characters know and what we know.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing operates on two speeds: the warehouse scenes (4, 11, 16, 21, 43, 45) unfold in agonizing real-time with long dialogue scenes and Mr. Orange's deterioration, while flashbacks provide kinetic relief—the getaway chase (scenes 6-9), the jewelry store robbery aftermath (scenes 42-43, 47-48). The Mr. Orange training montage (scenes 25-32) uses rapid cutting between rehearsal and performance. The script's longest sustained sequence is the warehouse confrontation beginning at scene 43, which builds for 13 pages to the final Mexican standoff. Tarantino deliberately slows down before violence (Mr. Blonde's torture setup in scene 22) and accelerates through action, creating a rhythm of dread and release.

incitingIncident.description: Mr. White carries the bleeding Mr. Orange into an empty warehouse, lays him on a mattress, and cradles him while promising Joe will arrive soon to help.

validation.ruling: Scene #3 is merely the getaway aftermath. Scene #4 (p16, 17%) is the true inciting incident: Mr. White carries the bleeding Mr. Orange into the warehouse rendezvous point, establishing the central dramatic situation—a botched heist, wounded accomplice, and the confined pressure-cooker setting where the entire mystery will unfold. This irreversibly shatters the status quo and forces all characters into the main conflict.

midpoint.description: Holdaway, a tough-looking black man in a Chairman Mao cap and military jacket, sits alone at Denny's eating a burger with ketchup-covered fries when Freddy Newendyke (Mr. Orange) in a letterman jacket enters and heads toward him.

validation.ruling: Scene #22 is a flashback showing Freddy's recruitment—backstory, not a present-action turning point. Scene #21 (p55, 57%) is the true midpoint: Mr. Orange shoots and kills Mr. Blonde, revealing himself as the undercover cop through decisive action. This is the point of no return that shifts the power dynamics, raises stakes exponentially, and transforms Mr. Orange from passive victim to active agent who can no longer hide.

climax.description: Eddie, Mr. White, and Mr. Pink enter the warehouse to find Mr. Blonde shot dead and the cop slumped over, and when Mr. Orange claims Blonde went crazy and was pulling a burn to steal the diamonds, Eddie shoots Mr. Orange while Mr. White shoots Eddie, then Joe enters and shoots Mr. White who shoots Joe, leaving Mr. Pink to grab the diamonds and flee as police sirens approach.

validation.ruling: Scene #45 (p88, 92%) is the climax: Eddie, Mr. White, and Mr. Pink discover the carnage, Eddie kills the cop, Mr. Orange accuses the dead Mr. Blonde of planning to betray them all, and the final confrontation begins that will resolve all tensions and reveal Mr. Orange's true identity. This is the peak dramatic confrontation where all narrative threads converge.

scenes.heading: INT. UNCLE BOB'S PANCAKE HOUSE - MORNING 1 1
scenes.summary: Eight men in black suits sit at a breakfast cafe discussing Madonna's 'Like a Virgin' while Joe flips through an address book and tries to remember who Toby is.

scenes.heading: EXT. UNCLE BOB'S PANCAKE HOUSE - DAY 2 2
scenes.summary: Over black screen, someone screams in agony while a car speeds through traffic, and the screaming person says he can't believe she killed him.

scenes.heading: INT. GETAWAY GAR (MOVING) - DAY 3 3
scenes.summary: Mr. White drives the getaway car at high speed while Mr. Orange lies bleeding in the backseat from a gunshot wound to the stomach, crying and scared.
scenes.turningPointNote: First revelation of the botched robbery — Mr. Orange is critically wounded and the heist has gone catastrophically wrong.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - DAY 4 4
scenes.summary: Mr. White carries the bleeding Mr. Orange into an empty warehouse, lays him on a mattress, and cradles him while promising Joe will arrive soon to help.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM HALLWAY - DAY 5 5
scenes.summary: In the bathroom hallway, Mr. Pink tells Mr. White he had a bad feeling about the job and believes they were set up, while Mr. White tells him to splash water on his face and stay cool.

scenes.heading: EXT. CROWDED CITY STREET - DAY 6 6
scenes.summary: Mr. Pink runs down a crowded sidewalk carrying a canvas bag and a magnum while four policemen chase him, and he collides with a rollerskating woman listening to a walkman.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR (STOPPED) - DAY 7 7
scenes.summary: Mr. Pink pulls himself off a car hood, points his magnum at the shocked female driver, demands she get out, and smashes the driver's side window when she won't unlock the door.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - DAY 8 8
scenes.summary: Mr. Pink drags the woman from her car, fires three shots at the approaching cops causing everyone to scatter, then hops in the car as cops fire back.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR (MOVING) - DAY 9 9
scenes.summary: Mr. Pink floors the stolen car and speeds down the street while cops fire after him.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - DAY 10 10
scenes.summary: Mr. Pink and Mr. White discuss the robbery in the bathroom, revealing Mr. Pink tagged a couple cops, Mr. White killed a few, and both express shock at Mr. Blonde's insane behavior during the heist.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - DAY 11 11
scenes.summary: Mr. Pink and Mr. White rush to Mr. Orange when he stops moaning, discover he's passed out but alive, then argue about whether to take him to a hospital or stay at the warehouse.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - DAY - MEDIUM C.U. ON MR. BLONDE 12 12
scenes.summary: Mr. Blonde enters eating a hot dog and drinking a coke, silently walks around the warehouse ignoring Mr. Pink's questions about what happened and where Mr. Blue is, then bends over Mr. Orange.

scenes.heading: EXT. WAREHOUSE - DAY 13 13
scenes.summary: Outside the warehouse, Mr. Blonde tells Mr. White and Mr. Pink they're waiting for Nice Guy Eddie, revealing he just talked to Eddie who's on his way.

scenes.heading: INT. JOE CABOT'S OFFICE - DAY 14 14
scenes.summary: In Joe Cabot's office, Joe finishes a phone call with Sid, then greets Toothpick Vic Vega (Mr. Blonde) who has just been released from prison after four years, and the two men embrace warmly.

scenes.heading: INT. NICE GUY EDDIE'S CAR (MOVING) - DAY 15 15
scenes.summary: Nice Guy Eddie drives while talking on his car phone about a major situation, telling someone named Dov he needs to talk with his father to find out what to do.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - DAY 16 16
scenes.summary: Mr. White, Mr. Pink, and Mr. Blonde surround a cop with his hands cuffed behind his back and beat him while music plays over the soundtrack.

scenes.heading: EXT. WAREHOUSE - DAY 20 20
scenes.summary: Eddie arrives at the warehouse, looks at the parked cars, calls the others assholes to himself, then bangs open the front door and steps inside.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - DAY 21 21
scenes.summary: Eddie enters to find the robbers beating the tied-up cop, sees the wounded Mr. Orange, and argues with Mr. Pink about whether they were set up, insisting nobody betrayed them and the cops showed up because they turned the store into a wild west show.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - DAY - MR. BLONDE AND COP 22 22
scenes.summary: Mr. Blonde closes the door after the others leave, turns toward the cop, and tells him they're alone at last while the cop insists he knows nothing about a setup.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. WAREHOUSE - DAY - HANDHELD SHOT 23 23
scenes.summary: Mr. Blonde walks out of the warehouse to his car, opens the trunk, pulls out a large can of gasoline, and walks back inside carrying it.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - DAY 24 24
scenes.summary: Mr. Blonde pours gasoline all over the begging cop while singing along to music, lights a match and moves it toward the cop, but is shot in the chest by Mr. Orange who empties his weapon into Mr. Blonde until he falls dead.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mr. Orange kills Mr. Blonde to save the cop, revealing Mr. Orange is not who he appears to be and fundamentally shifting the power dynamics.

scenes.heading: INT. DENNY'S - NIGHT 25 25
scenes.summary: Holdaway, a tough-looking black man in a Chairman Mao cap and military jacket, sits alone at Denny's eating a burger with ketchup-covered fries when Freddy Newendyke (Mr. Orange) in a letterman jacket enters and heads toward him.
scenes.turningPointNote: First reveal that Mr. Orange is actually undercover cop Freddy Newendyke working with police handler Holdaway.

scenes.heading: EXT. DENNY'S - NIGHT 26 26
scenes.summary: Through the restaurant window, Freddy slides into the booth across from Holdaway and talks while we cannot hear what they're saying.

scenes.heading: INT. DENNY'S - NIGHT 27 27
scenes.summary: Freddy tells Holdaway that Nice Guy Eddie picked him up after three days of waiting and took him to the Boots and Socks bar in Gardena where he met Joe and Mr. White, receiving the code name Mr. Orange.

scenes.heading: INT. MEN'S ROOM - L.A. TRAIN STATION - NIGHT 28 28
scenes.summary: In a train station men's room, Holdaway gives Freddy stapled papers containing an anecdote about a drug deal to memorize, explaining an undercover cop must be a great actor and this story is like a joke to make him seem authentic.

scenes.heading: INT. FREDDY'S APARTMENT - DAY 29 29
scenes.summary: Freddy paces in his apartment rehearsing the anecdote about a marijuana drought and his connection with a hippie chick in Santa Cruz, reading from the page and occasionally stumbling over words.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKING LOT - DAY 30 30
scenes.summary: Freddy performs the anecdote to Holdaway in a parking lot, continuing the story about people constantly calling him to buy weed during the drought.

scenes.heading: INT. BOOTS AND SOCKS BAR - NIGHT 31 31
scenes.summary: At the Boots and Socks bar, Freddy tells Joe, Nice Guy Eddie, and Mr. White the story about his friend's sister needing someone to accompany her with weed because her brother is in jail on traffic warrants, and the crooks enjoy the tale.

scenes.heading: INT. MEN'S ROOM - TRAIN STATION - DAY 32 32
scenes.summary: Freddy walks into a train station men's room carrying a bag and freezes when he sees six Los Angeles County Sheriffs and a German Shepherd all staring at him, while his voiceover explains they weren't waiting for him but stopped talking when he entered.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE - DAY 35 35
scenes.summary: Freddy and Holdaway look at a mug shot of Mr. White in an office, and Holdaway asks computer operator Jodie Seigel to pull up information on Lawrence Dimick.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLDAWAY'S OFFICE - DAY 36 36
scenes.summary: In Holdaway's office, Jodie reads Lawrence Dimick's criminal record to Freddy and Holdaway, detailing his two convictions for armed robbery and explaining he's a professional who rarely gets caught.

scenes.heading: INT. FREDDY'S APARTMENT - DAY 35 35
scenes.summary: Freddy answers his ringing phone and Nice Guy Eddie tells him it's time to grab his jacket.

scenes.heading: INT. NICE GUY EDDIE'S CAR (PARKED) - DAY 36 36
scenes.summary: Nice Guy Eddie speaks into his car phone telling Freddy they're parked outside, and Freddy says he'll be right down.

scenes.heading: INT. FREDDY'S APARTMENT - DAY 39 39
scenes.summary: Freddy hops around his apartment getting ready, puts on his jacket and sneakers, reaches for the door knob but stops, tells himself not to pussy out because they don't know anything and he's super cool, then exits.

scenes.heading: EXT. FREDDY'S APARTMENT - DAY 40 40
scenes.summary: Two cops in an unmarked car watch Freddy walk out of his building and get into Eddie's car, commenting on the courage required for undercover work, then follow the car into traffic.

scenes.heading: INT. NICE GUY EDDIE'S CAR (MOVING) - DAY 41 41
scenes.summary: In Eddie's moving car, Mr. Pink argues that black women won't tolerate what white women will and have a line they won't let men cross, while Mr. White sarcastically challenges this claim.

scenes.heading: EXT. WAREHOUSE - DAY 42 42
scenes.summary: Nice Guy Eddie pulls up outside the warehouse and the four men climb out of the car and follow Eddie inside.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - DAY 43 43
scenes.summary: The four men enter the warehouse where Joe is telling a joke to Mr. Blonde, Mr. Brown, and Mr. Blue, then Joe greets the arrivals and introduces Freddy as Mr. Orange to the crew.

scenes.heading: EXT. BLEACHERS - DAY 44 44
scenes.summary: On empty bleachers, Holdaway tells Freddy that police will be stationed across from the jewelry store with orders not to move in unless the robbery gets out of control, and men will watch the warehouse exterior but can't see inside.

scenes.heading: EXT. KARINA'S FINE JEWELRY - DAY 45 45
scenes.summary: Outside the jewelry store, customers and clerks are visible through windows while Mr. White and Freddy's voices discuss the robbery plan over the soundtrack.

scenes.heading: INT. MR. WHITE'S CAR (PARKED) - DAY 46 46
scenes.summary: Mr. White and Freddy sit in a parked car across from the jewelry store going over the plan, with Mr. White explaining the manager should give up the diamonds easily because the store is insured.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALLEY - DAY 47 47
scenes.summary: In an alley during the robbery, chaos erupts with gunfire and screaming in the distance, then a car whips around the corner and Freddy and Mr. White jump out to find the bloody, screaming Mr. Brown falling from the driver's seat claiming he's blind.
scenes.turningPointNote: The robbery goes catastrophically wrong — Mr. Brown is critically wounded and the crew is under fire, marking the complete collapse of the heist.

scenes.heading: INT. GETAWAY CAR (MOVING) - DAY 48 48
scenes.summary: In the moving getaway car, Freddy holds his stomach doubled over in pain and crying, replaying the earlier scene where he tells Mr. White he can't believe she killed him.

scenes.heading: INT. NICE GUY EDDIE'S CAR (MOVING) - DAY 49 49
scenes.summary: Mr. Pink drives while Eddie examines the diamonds in the passenger seat and Mr. White sits in back, with Eddie noting they got away with over two million dollars despite the mess and promising a woman named Bonnie will help Mr. Orange.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE - DAY 50 50
scenes.summary: Eddie, Mr. White, and Mr. Pink enter the warehouse to find Mr. Blonde shot dead and the cop slumped over, and when Mr. Orange claims Blonde went crazy and was pulling a burn to steal the diamonds, Eddie shoots Mr. Orange while Mr. White shoots Eddie, then Joe enters and shoots Mr. White who shoots Joe, leaving Mr. Pink to grab the diamonds and flee as police sirens approach.
scenes.turningPointNote: The final bloodbath — mutual accusations of betrayal lead to a Mexican standoff where Eddie, Joe, and Mr. White all shoot each other, the truth about Mr. Orange is revealed, and the crew destroys itself.
